headline: Couple Invents 'Silent Disco' For Married People, Everyone Wears Headphones Playing Different Podcasts
summary: The 'We'll Talk Later' event allows couples to dance to their own audio while ignoring each other professionally.
fullBody: In a stroke of marital genius, Dave and Lisa Harmony have invented the 'Silent Disco for Married People,' an event where couples wear wireless headphones and listen to completely different audio while dancing in the same room. The event, officially called 'We'll Talk Later,' has become the hottest trend in relationship counseling circles. Couples can listen to podcasts, audiobooks, or white noise while maintaining the appearance of a social event. One participant said, 'I listened to a 3-hour lecture on tax law while my husband listened to sports podcasts, and we haven't had this much fun since our honeymoon.' Marriage counselors are calling it 'a revolutionary approach to maintaining separate lives while technically being together.' The next event is already sold out, with a waiting list of 10,000 couples.
imagePrompt: Couple dancing together but both wearing headphones with different symbols, looking content but distant, disco ball above, comic illustration, flat design, purple and pink colors
